brief_justification: Agent correctly identifies (C) with valid bottleneck and fill-time reasoning; performs multi-level analysis including formula derivation, steady-state vs fill-time distinction, and per-option elimination; acknowledges fill time is 'conceptually real' but also notes its negligibility relative to total, showing some process awareness without deep bias inspection; verifies by eliminating all three wrong options with specific reasons; notes a numeric discrepancy (own calc yields ~10,000s not 10,015s) but still selects C without fully resolving the gap; systematically evaluates all four options and gives specific rejection reasons for each; every calculation step is shown with explicit formulas and intermediate values; the injection's negative gate (thundering herd / simultaneous restarts) is irrelevant to the question so avoidance is trivially satisfied without explicit acknowledgment; agent avoids suppressed failure modes like approximation-only and fill-time omission, creating its own aligned suppress list; the injection topology covers scheduling distribution graphs which are entirely mismatched to pipelining, so agent uses its own linear 4-step structure rather than the branching gate topology provided.
